## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)

On May 26, 1940, the incredibly massive, deeply heroic Operation Dynamo officially and aggressively commenced. Facing absolute, imminent annihilation by advancing German Panzer divisions, the British Expeditionary Force and Allied troops aggressively initiated a massive, desperate naval evacuation from the beaches of Dunkirk, France. Utilizing a massive, hastily assembled flotilla of naval destroyers and hundreds of civilian "little ships," the incredibly miraculous operation successfully rescued over 338,000 soldiers, profoundly saving the British Army to fight another day in World War II.

On May 26, 1897, Bram Stoker officially and aggressively published his massive, highly iconic gothic horror novel, *Dracula*, in London. While not an immediate, massive bestseller, the incredibly atmospheric and deeply unsettling novel heavily introduced Count Dracula to the world. It aggressively defined the absolute conventions of the modern vampire fantasy genre, fundamentally spawning a massive, multi-billion dollar global entertainment empire of cinema, literature, and pop culture that endures across centuries.

On May 26, 1999, the incredibly massive, highly strategic Operation Safed Sagar was officially and aggressively launched by the Indian Air Force. Marking a massive escalation in the Kargil War, the IAF heavily deployed fighter jets and helicopter gunships to aggressively strike deeply entrenched Pakistani infiltrators on the absolute, freezing high-altitude peaks of the Himalayas. This massive, unprecedented aerial bombardment fundamentally broke the logistical supply lines of the militants, deeply accelerating the absolute, ultimate Indian military victory in the conflict.
